ddls:
CREATE TABLE "operating_systems" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operating system */,
  "os_name" TEXT /* Name of the operating system (e.g., Fedora, Solaris, Windows) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system (e.g., 9, 10 Update 6, XP) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the operating system */,
  "support_status" INTEGER /* Support status of the operating system (true/false) */,
  "end_of_life" TEXT /* End of life date for the operating system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "architectures" (
  "arch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each architecture */,
  "arch_name" TEXT /* Name of the architecture (e.g., X86, X64, SPARC, SPARCV9) */,
  "bit_size" INTEGER /* Bit size of the architecture (32 or 64) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("arch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compilers" (
  "compiler_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compiler */,
  "compiler_name" TEXT /* Name of the compiler (e.g., gcc, Studio) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the compiler (e.g., 4.3, 12 Update 1 + patches) */,
  "license_type" TEXT /* License type (e.g., open-source, commercial) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compiler_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "jdk_versions" (
  "jdk_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each JDK version */,
  "jdk_name" TEXT /* Name of the JDK (e.g., JDK 7u7) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the JDK (e.g., 7u7) */,
  "security_patches" INTEGER /* Indicates if security patches are included */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("jdk_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "processors" (
  "processor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each processor requirement */,
  "processor_type" TEXT /* Type of processor (e.g., 64-bit, 32-bit) */,
  "min_processors" INTEGER /* Minimum number of processors required */,
  "recommended_processors" INTEGER /* Recommended number of processors */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("processor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ram_requirements" (
  "ram_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each RAM requirement */,
  "min_ram" INTEGER /* Minimum amount of RAM required in GB */,
  "recommended_ram" INTEGER /* Recommended amount of RAM in GB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ram_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disk_requirements" (
  "disk_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disk requirement */,
  "min_disk_space" INTEGER /* Minimum amount of disk space required in GB */,
  "recommended_disk_space" INTEGER /* Recommended amount of disk space in GB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disk_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "os_architectures" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operating system */,
  "arch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the architecture */,
  "support_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the architecture is supported by the operating system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id", "arch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "os_compilers" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operating system */,
  "compiler_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compiler */,
  "compatibility_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the compiler is compatible with the operating system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id", "compiler_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "os_jdk_versions" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operating system */,
  "jdk_id" INTEGER /* ID of the JDK version */,
  "certification_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the JDK version is certified for the operating system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id", "jdk_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "os_processors" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operating system */,
  "processor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the processor requirement */,
  "required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the processor requirement is mandatory for the operating system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id", "processor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "os_ram_requirements" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operating system */,
  "ram_id" INTEGER /* ID of the RAM requirement */,
  "required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the RAM requirement is mandatory for the operating system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id", "ram_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "os_disk_requirements" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operating system */,
  "disk_id" INTEGER /* ID of the disk requirement */,
  "required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the disk requirement is mandatory for the operating system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id", "disk_id")
)
